## Performance

### Standard Cardinality (5M rows)

### Correctness

- Polars: pass (10/10)
- Booster: pass (10/10)


## Single-Key `std` Evidence

`std` scale on the Rust-first path because each worker accumulates mergeable `(count, mean, m2)` state and Rayon only merges those partial states at the end.
When the dataset is smaller or the group count is low, the fixed overhead from NumPy contiguity/copy work, the Python↔Rust boundary, and Series materialization eats into that gain even though the kernel itself remains parallel.

| Workload | Agg | Sort | Backend | Execution | Cold | Warm |
|----------|-----|------|---------|-----------|------|------|
| standard | `std` | True | pandas | `pandas.groupby.std` | 38.19 ± 0.00 (38.19..38.19) | 39.34 ± 0.00 (39.34..39.34) |
| standard | `std` | True | polars | `polars.group_by.agg(std)` | 31.42 ± 0.00 (31.42..31.42) | 27.28 ± 0.00 (27.28..27.28) |
| standard | `std` | True | booster | `booster->rust.groupby_std_f64_sorted` | 8.56 ± 0.00 (8.56..8.56) | 4.91 ± 0.00 (4.91..4.91) |

### Booster conversion vs compute breakdown

The table below isolates the Rust-first Booster path for the same single-key datasets. `local_build`, `merge`, `reorder`, and `materialize` come from the internal Rust profiling hook, while the Python-side phases measure post-kernel normalization and final pandas Series assembly.

| Workload | Agg | Sort | Execution | Prepare inputs | Local build | Merge | Reorder | Materialize | Python normalize | Series build | Rust total | Total pipeline | Partial groups | Final groups | Partial/final |
|----------|-----|------|-----------|----------------|-------------|-------|---------|-------------|------------------|--------------|------------|----------------|----------------|--------------|---------------|
| standard | `std` | True | `booster->rust.groupby_std_f64_sorted` | 0.01 ± 0.00 (0.01..0.01) | 5.04 ± 0.00 (5.04..5.04) | 0.30 ± 0.00 (0.30..0.30) | 0.03 ± 0.00 (0.03..0.03) | 0.04 ± 0.00 (0.04..0.04) | 0.01 ± 0.00 (0.01..0.01) | 0.27 ± 0.00 (0.27..0.27) | 5.41 ± 0.00 (5.41..5.41) | 7.06 ± 0.00 (7.06..7.06) | 39,000 | 1,000 | 39.000 |
